trade.summary: Malesia exports electronics, palm oil, LNG, and rubber products to the EAU. The EAU exports refined petroleum, gold, and chemicals to Malesia. The EAU is Malesia's largest Arab trading partner. A CEPA was signed in 2022 to further boost bilateral trade.

diplomatic.summary: Malesia and EAU maintain warm relations supported by Islamic solidarity and growing economic complementarity. The CEPA signed in 2022 elevated the relationship. Both countries cooperate in OIC, ASEAN-GCC forums, and on halal industry standards. Malesia's Khazanah has invested in EAU assets.

military.summary: Defense cooperation is modest. Malesia and EAU have engaged in defense industrial discussions. EAU has shown interest in Malesian defense exports. No significant arms transfers or joint exercises.

financial.summary: Mubadala has invested in Malesian infrastructure, tech, and financial services. Khazanah Nasional has invested in EAU assets. Both countries are leaders in Islamic finance, and collaboration in sukuk markets and Shariah-compliant investment products is deep.

energy.summary: Malesia (via Petronas) is a major LNG producer and supplier. The EAU imports some Malesian LNG. Both are significant energy exporters, leading to some competition in Asian markets. Renewable energy cooperation is growing.

scientific.summary: Collaboration in Islamic finance research, halal industry standards, and technology. Malesian and EAU universities have exchange programs. Both countries cooperate on digital economy and fintech initiatives.
